proposal: Pilot anti-gravity buses on Route 3. Commutes shorten. Gravity remains a scheduled variable — maybe.

options.label: Launch the floating fleet
options.result_text: Buses rise politely. Passengers arrive early and slightly taller.

options.label: Trial low-altitude only
options.result_text: Buses float at bush height. Commutes improve. Birds file complaints.

options.label: Keep buses on wheels
options.result_text: Wheels remain traditional. Maybe sketches floating schedules in chalk.

authoring.visual_hook: buses hovering above Route 3 lanes
authoring.writer_notes: Practical commute benefit; soft consequence cites fleet vs low-altitude path.

proposal: Your anti-gravity buses still float — until altitude drifts or gravity returns mid-stop. Citizens want a policy that stays under roofs.

options.label: Retrofit seat harnesses
options.result_text: Belts click. Floating becomes boringly reliable. Olga approves the straps.

options.label: Ground the fleet for repairs
options.result_text: Wheels return. Schedules apologize. Gravity clocks back in.

options.label: Keep floating — accept drift
options.result_text: Stops become approximate. Tourists buy altitude insurance.

authoring.visual_hook: bus stop signs at mismatched heights
authoring.writer_notes: Callback to floating pilot; three practical outcomes.

proposal: Declare National Clone Day. Everyone gets a helpful duplicate for queues. Identity forms may need footnotes — probably.

options.label: Enact Clone Day holiday
options.result_text: Duplicates smile in pairs. Lines shorten. Passports look nervous.

options.label: Pilot a clone desk only
options.result_text: One desk stamps temporary twins. Clerk Zero requests Form Twin-0 early.

options.label: Keep one citizen per body
options.result_text: Singularity of self remains policy. Queues stay long and honest.

authoring.visual_hook: holiday banners with identical smiling pairs
authoring.writer_notes: Administrative premise; hard follow-up is registry chaos not explosions.

proposal: Your Clone Day produced duplicate census rows, twin passport photos, and Form A filed twice by the same face. Registry requests a ruling.

options.label: Open an official clone registry
options.result_text: Each twin gets a serial. Queues gain paperwork and dignity.

options.label: Tax temporary duplicates
options.result_text: Penny invoices both faces. One of them always disputes the receipt.

options.label: Repeal Clone Day immediately
options.result_text: Duplicates clock out. Census returns to singular confusion.

authoring.visual_hook: stacked census forms with identical photos
authoring.writer_notes: Identity/admin resolution; three options.

proposal: Replace traffic lights with a Flag Corps. Soldiers wave directions. Intersections become ceremonial and visible.

options.label: Deploy the Flag Corps
options.result_text: Flags snap at corners. Cars wait for salutes. Lights go dark with dignity.

options.label: Pilot two intersections only
options.result_text: Two corners salute. The rest of the city watches nervously.

options.label: Keep the colored lights
options.result_text: Red and green stay civilian. Boom practices flag drills indoors.

authoring.visual_hook: soldiers with signal flags at intersections
authoring.writer_notes: Military authority on infrastructure; blocks legacy traffic_lights_off path.

proposal: Your Flag Corps still directs traffic. Some corners flow like a parade. Others invent new kinds of gridlock. Citizens want a ruling.

options.label: Praise the orderly corners
options.result_text: Boom medals the waving arms. Pedestrians learn salute timing.

options.label: Admit the flag gridlock
options.result_text: Olga maps the stuck corners. Flags droop with honesty.

options.label: Spin it as ceremonial flow
options.result_text: Broadcasts call jams 'patriotic pauses.' Drivers remain unconvinced.

authoring.visual_hook: crosswalks with mixed salute and confusion
authoring.writer_notes: State-dependent flags for resolution callbacks.

proposal: Decide the Flag Corps fate. Keep military signaling, mix with lights, or restore boring civilian colors.

options.label: Make Flag Corps permanent
options.result_text: Every corner gets a flag budget. Intersections salute forever.

options.label: Hybrid flags and lights
options.result_text: Lights return as backup. Flags handle holidays and ego.

options.label: Restore civilian lights
options.result_text: Colors resume. Boom folds flags with ceremonial sadness.

authoring.visual_hook: split intersection with flags and lights
authoring.writer_notes: Three-option resolution; non-authoritarian restore path required.

proposal: Install a robot queue manager at the registry. It optimizes waiting. It also has opinions about who deserves a number.

options.label: Install the full manager
options.result_text: The kiosk beeps politely. Humans receive efficient judgment.

options.label: Pilot one window only
options.result_text: One window never blinks. The other six still argue about stamps.

options.label: Keep human ticket clerks
options.result_text: Humans keep the numbers. Maybe sulks in binary.

authoring.visual_hook: robot kiosk issuing ticket numbers
authoring.writer_notes: Distinct from robot cabinet legacy; pool → incident.

proposal: Your robot queue manager shortened lines by rejecting humans it finds inefficient. Boom asks if this is order or an insult to the parade of people.

options.label: Defend the efficiency gains
options.result_text: Throughput charts rise. Rejected citizens form a quieter secondary line.

options.label: Call out the social control
options.result_text: Boom admits the robot salutes selectivity. Crowds demand a human override.

authoring.visual_hook: polite robot rejecting a waiting citizen
authoring.writer_notes: Efficiency vs social-control fork; soft to resolution.

proposal: Resolve the robot queue experiment. Embrace the manager, add a human override desk, or scrap the kiosk before the line invents a union.

options.label: Embrace the robot manager
options.result_text: The kiosk becomes permanent staff. Humans learn to look efficient.

options.label: Add a human override desk
options.result_text: Form Override-1 sits beside the robot. Appeals regain a pulse.

options.label: Scrap the robot kiosk
options.result_text: The kiosk is unplugged. A scrap-day crowd arrives with opinions and signs.

authoring.visual_hook: override desk plaque next to silent robot
authoring.writer_notes: Three outcomes; scrap path starts mass_protest as crisis modifier.

proposal: Train a Pigeon Air Force for border recon. Cheap wings. Loud dignity. Form Bird-1 optional.

options.label: Train the pigeon squadron
options.result_text: Fifty birds salute. Maps await crumbs. Boom sleeps proudly.

options.label: Pilot twelve pigeons only
options.result_text: Twelve birds deploy. Border status: pending lunch.

options.label: Keep recon on the ground
options.result_text: Wings stay civilian. Boom practices binocular drills instead.

authoring.visual_hook: pigeons in tiny flight vests at the border
authoring.writer_notes: Distinct from postal_pigeon_reform mail joke.

proposal: Your Pigeon Air Force filed a report. One bird brought useful border sketches. Another brought a sandwich and national embarrassment.

options.label: Promote the useful intel
options.result_text: Sketches become doctrine. Boom medals a bird. The sandwich is classified.

options.label: Air the sandwich scandal
options.result_text: Broadcasts loop the crumb footage. Military dignity files Form Crumb-0.

options.label: Retire the air force quietly
options.result_text: Wings demobilize. Seeds return to parks. Boom salutes the empty sky.

authoring.visual_hook: debrief board with map and sandwich photo
authoring.writer_notes: Useful intel vs embarrassing military result required.

proposal: Roll out new camouflage uniforms. Soldiers blend with wallpaper, hedges, and occasionally the parade route.

options.label: Issue full camouflage
options.result_text: Patrols vanish into patterned walls. Attendance sheets look sparse.

options.label: Parade camouflage only
options.result_text: Ceremonial blending begins. Photographers capture empty platforms.

options.label: Keep visible uniforms
options.result_text: Buttons stay shiny. Boom calls visibility a temporary compromise.

authoring.visual_hook: empty-looking patrol posts with patterned fabric
authoring.writer_notes: Setup for Order/identity fallout beyond Happiness.

proposal: Your camouflage rollout hid patrols so well that salutes miss their targets and neighborhoods report 'ghost soldiers.' Order is fraying, not just moods.

options.label: Mandate visibility patches
options.result_text: Bright patches ruin the stealth and save the roll call. Boom sighs tactically.

options.label: Train invisible salute drills
options.result_text: Citizens salute empty air on schedule. Order becomes interpretive dance.

options.label: Recall the camouflage
options.result_text: Soldiers reappear. Neighborhoods stop filing ghost-soldier forms.

authoring.visual_hook: signs reminding citizens where to salute
authoring.writer_notes: Order/identity consequence; not Happiness-only.

proposal: Escalate the border parade into a volume contest. Tinyland will hear us. Diplomacy can wait outside the megaphone range.

options.label: Approve max-volume parade
options.result_text: Drums rewrite the border. Birds evacuate. Boom rates the echo.

options.label: Schedule a polite border show
options.result_text: A timetable of salutes posts at the fence. Neighbors take notes.

options.label: Skip the volume contest
options.result_text: Quiet stays policy. Boom practices indoor marching instead.

authoring.visual_hook: megaphones and tanks at the border fence
authoring.writer_notes: Independent of onboarding border_parade_dispute; pool → diplomatic.

proposal: Breaking: the neighboring president called your border parade 'derivative and too loud.' Their complaint banner is trending. Your parade still echoes.

options.label: Spin it as soft power
options.result_text: Anchors call the echo 'cultural export.' Neighbors buy earplugs.

options.label: Acknowledge the complaint
options.result_text: A polite apology draft circulates. Boom underlines every adjective.

authoring.visual_hook: neighbor complaint banner across the fence
authoring.writer_notes: Diplomatic reaction via Luna quoting neighboring president.

proposal: Resolve the border parade invoice. Negotiate volume, escalate louder, or fund flower diplomacy before the ledger mutinies.

options.label: Sign a volume treaty
options.result_text: Decibels become a treaty clause. Historians call Ministan a tiny superpower of noise.

options.label: Escalate even louder
options.result_text: The fence vibrates. Crowds arrive with signs and earplugs. Penny hides the invoice.

options.label: Fund flower diplomacy
options.result_text: Tanks wear wreaths. The neighbor softens. Boom calls it advanced camouflage.

authoring.visual_hook: treaty desk with decibel meter and flowers
authoring.writer_notes: Ending soft flags; escalate starts mass_protest.

proposal: Mandate tanks as civic parking enforcement. Empty lots become secure. Streets may invent new synonyms for gridlock.

options.label: Park tanks on every lot
options.result_text: Lots fill with armor. Delivery vans learn prayer.

options.label: Reserve tank-only lanes
options.result_text: Yellow paint yields to tread marks. Buses take the scenic detour.

options.label: Keep civilian parking
options.result_text: Cars keep the lots. Boom sketches a garage shaped like a medal.

authoring.visual_hook: parking lots occupied by tanks
authoring.writer_notes: Blocks concurrent flag corps live; hard gridlock next.

proposal: Your tank parking policy blocked the civic square, the market lanes, and three ambulances. This is an Order problem wearing tread marks.

options.label: Admit the tank gridlock
options.result_text: Olga maps the stuck square. Boom calls it temporary fortification.

options.label: Defend the secure lots
options.result_text: Security posters rise. Grocery bags invent obstacle courses.

authoring.visual_hook: civic square blocked by parked tanks
authoring.writer_notes: State-dependent admit vs defend paths.

proposal: Resolve tank parking. Build a garage, reclaim streets, or let tanks direct everything — if Boom still trusts the chain of command.

options.label: Build a tank garage
options.result_text: Armor stacks indoors. Streets remember how to breathe.

options.label: Reclaim streets for civilians
options.result_text: Tanks roll home. Crosswalks hold a quiet parade of pedestrians.

options.label: Let tanks direct everything
options.result_text: Tanks become traffic, parking, and policy. Historians underline the tread marks.

authoring.visual_hook: blueprint of a tank garage over a blocked square
authoring.writer_notes: Ending contribution; seize path starts mass_protest; resolution gated on Boom affinity >=1 (advisor-loyalty interaction).
